evals.name: adi-singh-clean-demo
evals.prompt: Generate the YouTube package for this video. Deepgram transcript is at evals/files/Adi Singh_normalized.deepgram.corrected.json. It's a demo from MCP Night.
evals.expected_output: Full package: 5 titles, YT description with hook + chapter markers from PARAGRAPH_TIMESTAMPS + body + links, pinned comment, social caption ≤200 chars, LinkedIn (affirming), X (challenging). Chapter timestamps must match the parser's emitted timestamps. First chapter at 0:00.

evals.name: evan-bacon-with-links
evals.prompt: i need the youtube package for evan bacon's mcp night demo. transcript: evals/files/Evan Bacon_normalized.deepgram.corrected.json. announcement post is at https://workos.com/blog/expo-mcp. evan on x is @baconbrix.
evals.expected_output: Full package using only valid paragraph timestamps. Announcement URL and @baconbrix surface in the pinned comment and YouTube links section (not invented). Lowercase/casual prompt should still produce well-formatted output.

evals.name: madison-packer-no-context
evals.prompt: Publishing package for evals/files/Madison Packer_normalized.deepgram.corrected.json please
evals.expected_output: Minimal-context prompt should still produce a complete package, infer speaker/topic from the transcript, and silently omit any link items it can't fill in (no [add] placeholders).

evals.name: karen-serfaty-from-srt
evals.prompt: Generate the YouTube package for evals/files/Karen Serfaty.srt — it's Karen's MCP Night demo for AgentCard (https://www.agentcard.sh).
evals.expected_output: Exercises the SRT parser path. Output must be structurally identical to a Deepgram-driven run: same sections, same chapter timestamp discipline (pulled from the SRT parser's PARAGRAPH_TIMESTAMPS line), same placeholder rules.

evals.name: rhys-sullivan-from-srt
evals.prompt: Publishing package for evals/files/Rhys Sullivan.srt please. He's the founder of executor.sh (https://executor.sh).
evals.expected_output: Second SRT-path eval. Same constraints as eval 4. Verifies the SRT parser is robust to a different video's pacing and structure.
